blocks.text: DTC P1890: CVT Transmission function failure

The gearshift control is based on the basic shift map for each position stored in the PGM-FI ECU in advance. The PGM-FI ECU controls the drive pulley control SOL.V. and driven pulley control SOL.V. to set the drive pulley speed to the target value based on vehicle speed and throttle opening angle. V. and driven pulley control SOL.V. to change the oil pressure applied to the drive pulley and driven pulley, and feeds back the difference between the target drive pulley speed and the drive pulley speed during driving. When shifting in the low gear ratio direction, high hydraulic pressure is applied to the driven pulley to change the diameter of the driven pulley belt in the direction of expansion. When shifting in the high gear ratio direction, high hydraulic pressure is applied to the drive pulley to change the drive pulley belt diameter in the direction of expansion. If the select lever position is in-gear (driving force transmission state) and the difference between the target drive pulley speed and the drive pulley speed during driving is greater than the specified speed, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the CVT transmission function has failed and stores DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off, D indicator light: flashing
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
paddle shift switch car without equipment
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When the difference between the target drive pulley speed and the drive pulley speed during driving [drive pulley rpm] exceeds 500 rpm for more than 18 seconds.

items.text: Slowly accelerate to a vehicle speed of [vehicle speed] 60 km/h and run at a vehicle speed of [vehicle speed] 60 km/h for at least 60 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
